title: 10 best herbal teas for dry skin
intro_desc: Herbal teas for dry skin are infusions made from plants and flowers that are known to soothe, moisturize, and nourish dry skin. They are a natural and caffeine-free alternative to traditional teas. Some examples of herbal teas that help with dry skin include chamomile, calendula, and lavender. Chamomile tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry skin. Calendula tea is rich in antioxidants and has been shown to promote skin hydration and elasticity. Lavender tea has calming effects and can help soothe dry, itchy skin. Drinking these herbal teas can improve the lives of those with dry skin by providing a simple, cost-effective, and natural way to alleviate dryness and discomfort. Additionally, these teas can be used topically as a compress or added to bath water for an extra boost of hydration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: High mallow tea helps with dry skin because it contains mucilage, a substance that creates a protective barrier on the skin, helping it retain moisture. Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, it soothes irritation and reduces redness. High mallow tea also has essential fatty acids that nourish the skin, promoting elasticity and suppleness. Drinking high mallow tea or using it as a compress can provide relief from dry, itchy skin, leaving it feeling soft and hydrated.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: High mallow tea is rich in mucilage, a slippery substance that helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin.
Anti-inflammatory: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in high mallow tea have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry skin.

Antioxidant: High mallow tea contains high levels of antioxidants, such as vitamin C and E, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors, and promote skin health.
Soothing: The demulcent properties of high mallow tea help to soothe and protect dry, cracked skin by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface.
Emollient: High mallow tea is rich in emollients, such as omega-3 fatty acids, that help to soften and smooth dry, rough skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, the leaves help soothe dry skin and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for alleviating dry, irritated skin.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids, seeds help to restore the skin's natural barrier and lock in moisture.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of dried high mallow flowers and leaves with 4 cups of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove solid plant material, reserving the liquid.
Compost the solid plant material and let the tea cool to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1 cup of the high mallow tea, up to three times daily, for moisturizing benefits to dry skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with dry skin because it contains mucilage, a substance that becomes slippery and slimy when wet. This mucilage can help soothe and hydrate dry, irritated skin by forming a protective barrier that locks in moisture. Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, marshmallow tea can also help reduce redness and inflammation associated with dry skin. Furthermore, it has been suggested that the tea may help stimulate the production of collagen, a protein that helps keep skin strong and elastic. Drinking marshmallow tea or using it as a compress or topical treatment can provide relief and comfort for those suffering from dry, itchy, or inflamed skin.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Marshmallow tea contains mucilage, a gel-like substance that creates a protective barrier on the skin, helping to lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin.
Anti-inflammatory property: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in marshmallow tea have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing redness and irritation associated with dry, flaky skin.
Emollient property: The high mucilage content in marshmallow tea acts as an emollient, softening and smoothing the skin, making it an effective remedy for rough, dry patches.
Hydrating property: Marshmallow tea's hydrating properties help replenish the skin's natural moisture levels, restoring suppleness and elasticity to dry, dehydrated skin.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants in marshmallow tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the skin from environmental stressors and free radicals, promoting overall skin health and reducing the signs of aging in dry, mature skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, leaves help soothe dry skin and promote skin cell regeneration.
Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory properties, flowers aid in reducing redness and irritation associated with dry skin.
Buds: High in essential oils, buds provide moisture and nourishment to dry, flaky skin.
Stems: Packed with vitamins and minerals, stems support skin hydration and elasticity.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of dried marshmallow root, and 1 tablespoon of dried calendula flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the solid parts, then add 1 teaspoon of honey and 1/4 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers.
Stir well until the honey is fully dissolved.
Serve the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Great mullein tea helps with dry skin because it contains mucilage, a substance that has emollient properties, which can soothe and soften dry, irritated skin. The tea is also rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, and anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce redness and inflammation. Furthermore, great mullein tea has been shown to have hydrating effects, helping to lock in moisture and alleviate dryness. Drinking the tea or using it as a compress can provide relief and help improve the overall condition of dry, flaky skin.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Great mullein tea is an effective demulcent, which means it contains mucilage that creates a protective film on the skin, helping to lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin. This property is important for dry skin because it helps to rehydrate and nourish the skin, reducing flakiness and inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property are polysaccharides found in the mullein leaves.

Anti-inflammatory: Great mullein tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with dry skin. This property is important for dry skin because it alleviates discomfort and promotes skin healing. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins, flavonoids, and tannins found in the mullein plant.

Emollient: Great mullein tea has emollient properties, which help to soften and smooth dry, rough skin by filling in the gaps between skin cells. This property is important for dry skin because it enhances skin elasticity and suppleness, making the skin appear healthier and more youthful. The active constituents responsible for this property are the essential oils and fatty acids present in the mullein leaves.

Antioxidant: Great mullein tea contains antioxidants, which help protect the skin from free radical damage caused by environmental factors such as pollution and UV radiation. This property is important for dry skin because it helps maintain skin health and integrity, preventing premature aging and promoting skin repair. The active constituents responsible for this property are various phenolic compounds, including flavonoids and tannins, found in the mullein plant.

Astringent: Great mullein tea has astringent properties, which help to tighten and tone the skin, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. This property is important for dry skin because it helps to improve skin texture and firmness, making the skin look more radiant and refreshed. The active constituents responsible for this property are tannins and saponins found in the mullein leaves.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in mucilage, leaves help to soothe and hydrate dry skin.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and saponins, making them effective in treating dry, irritated skin.
Buds: High in antioxidants, buds aid in protecting and nourishing dry skin.
Seeds: Source of essential fatty acids, seeds assist in restoring skin's natural barrier and suppleness.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water and let it cool down for a few minutes until it's warm, but not boiling.
Add one teaspoon of dried mullein leaves to a tea infuser or tea bag.
Place the tea infuser or tea bag in a cup and pour the warm water over it.
Let the tea steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the herb to release its beneficial compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or tea bag and enjoy the herbal mullein tea, which can help soothe and moisturize dry skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tea helps with dry skin because it contains high levels of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that nourish and hydrate the skin. The plant's mucilage content creates a protective barrier on the skin, locking in moisture and soothing irritation. Additionally, plantain tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and inflammation associated with dry, itchy skin. Drinking plantain tea can also promote gut health, which is linked to skin health. By improving the body's ability to absorb nutrients and eliminate toxins, plantain tea can help maintain healthy, moisturized skin from the inside out.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like allantoin and rosmarinic acid, which help reduce skin redness and irritation caused by dryness.
Hydrating: Plantain tea is rich in mucilage, a substance that forms a protective film on the skin, helping to lock in moisture and soothe dry, flaky skin.

Antioxidant: Plantain tea contains high levels of antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which protect the skin from oxidative stress and help maintain its barrier function, preventing water loss.
Wound healing: Plantain tea has been shown to promote wound healing and tissue regeneration, thanks to compounds like allantoin and tannins, which can help repair dry, cracked skin.
Soothing: Plantain tea contains various soothing compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, which alleviate itching, reduce inflammation, and provide relief for dry, itchy skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and hydrating properties, plantain tea leaves can help soothe and moisturize dry skin.
Seeds: Packed with fatty acids and vitamins, plantain tea seeds can provide essential nutrients to help nourish and protect dry skin.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, plantain tea flowers can help reduce redness and irritation in dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried plantain leaves and rinse them under cold water to remove any impurities.
Place the leaves in a teapot and pour 8 ounces of boiling water over them, allowing the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, discarding them safely.
Add honey to taste, if desired, for a natural sweetener.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day for optimal benefits to dry skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with dry skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and emollient properties that soothe and moisturize the skin. The tea is rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, which have antioxidant effects that protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Moreover, pot marigold tea has been shown to stimulate the production of collagen, a protein that helps keep the skin firm and elastic. By promoting collagen production and reducing inflammation, pot marigold tea can help relieve dry skin, reduce redness and irritation, and improve the skin's overall health and appearance. Its soothing and moisturizing effects make it an ideal natural remedy for dry skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and irritation, important for dry skin as it often becomes inflamed. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids.
Emollient: The tea can help to soften and soothe dry skin, acting as a natural moisturizer. This is mainly due to the high content of mucilage in pot marigold.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tea has antimicrobial properties that can help protect dry skin from infection, which is important as dry skin can crack and allow bacteria to enter. This is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and essential oils.
Antioxidant: The tea contains antioxidants that help protect skin cells from damage, promoting skin health and regeneration. This is largely due to the high levels of carotenoids and flavonoids.
Circulation-boosting: Pot marigold tea can help improve blood flow to the skin, promoting skin cell regeneration and healing. This is thanks to the presence of essential oils and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in antioxidants and have emollient properties, making them ideal for soothing dry skin.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, making them suitable for treating dry skin.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain essential fatty acids that help to nourish and hydrate dry skin, promoting skin health.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried marigold petals and place them in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the marigold petals.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the petals.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tea helps with dry skin because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and tannins. These compounds can help to soothe and moisturize dry, irritated skin, while also reducing redness and inflammation. Additionally, comfrey tea is rich in allantoin, a compound that promotes skin cell regeneration and can help to repair damaged skin. This makes comfrey tea an excellent natural remedy for dry skin, as it not only hydrates and soothes the skin, but also helps to improve its overall health and appearance.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: Comfrey tea contains mucilage, a gel-like substance that helps to lock in moisture, keeping the skin hydrated and supple.
Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tea has anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of allantoin and rosmarinic acid, which can help soothe dry, irritated skin.

Skin cell regeneration: Comfrey tea promotes skin cell regeneration, as it contains allantoin, a compound that supports skin cell proliferation and tissue regeneration, aiding in the healing of dry, cracked skin.
Pain relief: Comfrey tea contains compounds like rosmarinic acid and symphytanin, which have analgesic properties that can help alleviate any discomfort associated with dry, itchy skin.

Antioxidant: Comfrey tea has antioxidant properties due to its polyphenol content, which can help protect the skin from environmental stressors and support overall skin health, reducing the appearance of dry skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients and mucilage, comfrey leaves help soothe and moisturize dry skin.
Rhyzomes: Containing allantoin, comfrey rhyzomes promote skin cell regeneration, aiding in the treatment of dry and damaged skin.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 tablespoon of dried comfrey leaf with 8 ounces of water.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat and let the tea steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the comfrey leaves.
Allow the tea to cool before drinking or using it as a topical treatment for dry skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime tea helps with dry skin because it is rich in antioxidants and vitamin C, which can improve the skin's barrier function and help retain moisture. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can also reduce redness and irritation associated with dry skin. Additionally, the herbal tea contains flavonoids and essential oils that have emollient and humectant properties, helping to soothe and hydrate the skin. Drinking large-leaved lime tea or using it as a compress or toner can provide relief and nourishment to dry, flaky, and itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: Large-leaved lime tea is rich in flavonoids and antioxidants that help to lock in moisture, keeping the skin hydrated and supple.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of large-leaved lime tea, due to its high polyphenol content, help to soothe dry and irritated skin.
Skin Barrier Repair: Large-leaved lime tea contains essential oils and vitamins that promote skin barrier repair, reducing water loss and improving skin elasticity.

Antioxidant-rich: The high antioxidant content, including vitamin C, found in large-leaved lime tea, aids in collagen production and helps protect the skin from environmental stressors that can exacerbate dry skin.
Soothing: Large-leaved lime tea has natural cooling and calming effects, alleviating dry, itchy skin, and providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and tannins, large-leaved lime leaves help soothe dry skin and promote healing.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, lime flowers can help reduce redness and irritation in dry skin.
Bark: A source of essential oils, lime bark can help moisturize and protect dry skin from environmental stressors.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, lime seeds can help nourish and hydrate dry skin, improving its overall texture and appearance.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and steep 2 teaspoons of large-leaved lime tea for 5-7 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried lemon balm to the boiling water before steeping for its skin-soothing properties.
After steeping, remove tea and herbs, then stir in 1 teaspoon of raw honey for added skin-nourishing benefits.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for best results in improving dry skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden tea helps with dry skin because it contains flavonoids and mucilaginous substances that have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. These compounds can help soothe irritated skin, reduce redness and inflammation, and lock in moisture to keep the skin hydrated and supple. Additionally, littleleaf linden tea has been shown to improve blood circulation, which can help promote the delivery of nutrients and oxygen to the skin, further supporting its health and vitality. Drinking littleleaf linden tea regularly can help keep the skin nourished and moisturized, providing relief from dryness and promoting a healthy, radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Littleleaf linden tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and irritation, important for dry skin as it often becomes inflamed and sensitive. This property is attributed to the flavonoids and volatile oils present in the tea.

Emollient: The tea acts as an emollient, helping to lock in moisture and soothe dry, rough skin. This is due to the mucilage content in littleleaf linden, which creates a protective barrier on the skin.

Antioxidant: Littleleaf linden tea contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dry skin conditions. The antioxidant properties come from the flavonoids and phenolic acids found in the tea.

Circulation-boosting: The tea can help improve blood circulation, which aids in delivering essential nutrients and oxygen to the skin, promoting healthy skin cell function and regeneration. This is attributed to the flavonoids and essential oils in littleleaf linden.

Calming: Littleleaf linden tea has calming properties that can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can indirectly benefit dry skin by preventing stress-induced skin issues. This is due to the soothing effects of the volatile oils and flavonoids found in the tea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and tannins, littleleaf linden leaves help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness.
Flowers: High in flavonoids, littleleaf linden flowers have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate dry skin and promote skin hydration.
Bark: Containing mucilaginous compounds, littleleaf linden bark can create a soothing barrier for dry skin, helping to retain moisture.
Buds: Packed with active compounds, littleleaf linden buds provide a potent source of skin-nourishing antioxidants, supporting dry skin health.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried littleleaf linden flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 2-3 minutes.
Pour the hot water over the linden flowers and steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the flowers.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with dry skin because it has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. The tea is rich in antioxidants, including flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Chamomile also contains azulene, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin. Furthermore, German chamomile tea is a natural emollient, which means it helps to lock in moisture and keep the skin hydrated. Drinking chamomile tea or using it as a compress can help to relieve dry, itchy, and flaky skin, leaving it feeling soft, smooth, and supple.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea contains the anti-inflammatory compound chamazulene, which helps reduce skin inflammation and irritation, providing relief for dry, itchy skin.
Moisturizing: The antioxidant and essential oil content, including bisabolol and azulene, found in German chamomile tea, helps to lock in moisture, keeping dry skin hydrated and supple.
Soothing: German chamomile tea has a calming effect on the skin due to its flavonoid and terpenoid content, which soothes irritated, dry skin and promotes skin cell regeneration.

Wound healing: The flavonoids and essential oils present in German chamomile tea have been shown to promote wound healing, which can help dry skin recover from damage and reduce the appearance of flaky, dry patches.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of German chamomile tea, attributed to its essential oil content, can help protect the skin from harmful bacteria, reducing the risk of skin infections and irritations that can exacerbate dry skin conditions.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile flowers help soothe dry and irritated skin.
Leaves: Containing high levels of antioxidants, chamomile leaves aid in protecting and nourishing dry skin.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils, chamomile seeds help moisturize and rejuvenate dry and flaky skin.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers.
Place the chamomile flowers in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil water and pour it over the chamomile flowers, allowing them to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, 2-3 times a day for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with dry skin because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that soothe and hydrate the skin. The herb is rich in azulene, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation, making it an effective treatment for dry and itchy skin. Additionally, yarrow tea is packed with antioxidants that protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, helping to keep the skin healthy and moisturized. Drinking yarrow tea or using it as a compress can help to alleviate dryness, redness, and flakiness, leaving the skin feeling soft, smooth, and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea helps reduce inflammation, which is important for dry skin as it can soothe irritation and redness caused by skin dryness. This property is attributed to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Astringent: Yarrow tea has astringent properties that help tighten skin and reduce oiliness, which can be beneficial for dry skin as it can help restore the skin's natural barrier function. The astringent effect is due to the tannins present in yarrow.

Moisturizing: Yarrow tea can help moisturize the skin, which is crucial for dry skin as it can help alleviate symptoms of dryness, flaking, and itching. This is due to the emollient properties of yarrow, which help lock in moisture and keep the skin hydrated.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent skin infections, which is important for dry skin as it can be more prone to irritation and infection. The antimicrobial effect is attributed to the presence of volatile oils like azulene and camphor.

Antioxidant: Yarrow tea contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from free radical damage, which is important for dry skin as it can help prevent further damage and promote skin health. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other antioxidant compounds found in yarrow.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain flavonoids and terpene compounds that have anti-inflammatory and moisture-retaining properties, making them ideal for soothing dry skin.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants that help protect the skin from environmental stressors, promoting healthy and hydrated skin.
Seeds: Contain essential fatty acids and nutrients that nourish and lock in moisture, helping to alleviate dryness and improve skin elasticity.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for a minute or two, then add 1-2 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers and leaves.
Steep the mixture in a covered container for 5-10 minutes to allow the herb to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the solid plant material.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while warm, 2-3 times a day, for best results in soothing and moisturizing dry skin.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dry skin with herbal teas is by using them as a compress or in the bath. Steep a strong tea from herbs known to soothe and moisturize, such as chamomile, calendula, or lavender, and let it cool. For a compress, soak a clean cloth in the tea and apply to the affected areas. For a bath, add several tea bags or loose tea to the running water. These herbal teas help restore the skin's natural barrier, reducing dryness and leaving skin soft and supple.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry skin that are treated with herbal teas include eczema and dandruff. Chamomile tea is often used as a compress to soothe eczema symptoms, while nettle tea can be consumed to alleviate internal inflammation that may contribute to skin conditions. For dandruff, sage tea can be used as a rinse to soothe an itchy scalp and combat flaking. Additionally, burdock root tea can help improve liver function, which can in turn promote healthy skin. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment regimen.
